title: Validate an email (simply)
prompt_md:
Backend validation should never trust the client. Write `is_valid_email(s)` returning `True` only when ALL of these hold, else `False`:

- the string contains **exactly one** `"@"`
- the part **before** the `@` (the local part) is non-empty
- the part **after** the `@` (the domain) is non-empty
- the domain contains a `"."`

Keep it simple and deterministic — this is not a full RFC email parser.
hints: After `local, domain = s.split('@')`, test `local`, `domain`, and `'.' in domain`.
